beginner.question: Lesson 1: Verb To Be. Questions about am, is, are.
Lesson 2: Subject Pronouns. Questions about I, you, he, she, it, we, they.
Sara is my sister. ___ is a student.
Lesson 3: Articles. Questions about a and an.
Lesson 4: Simple Present. Questions about present simple tense.
Lesson 5: Basic Vocabulary. Questions about everyday words.

intermediate.question: Section 1: Past Simple and Present Continuous. Choose the correct verb form.
She __________ dinner when I called her.
Section 2: Comparatives and Prepositions. Choose the correct comparative form or preposition.
This restaurant is __________ than the one near my school.
Section 3: Vocabulary. Daily Routine, Travel, Food, and Feelings.
He always __________ to school by bus.
She __________ lunch at noon every day.
Section 4: Question Forms. Choose the correct question structure.
Section 5: Reading Comprehension. Listen to the texts and answer the questions.

Reading Text 1: Ali usually wakes up at 7 a.m. He has breakfast at 7:30. After that, he goes to school by bus. At school, he studies English, Math, and Science. After school, he goes home and does his homework. At the moment, Ali is reading a book.
What time does Ali wake up?
How does he go to school?
Reading Text 2: Yesterday, Sara went to the supermarket. She bought apples, bread, and milk. At the moment, she is cooking dinner for her family.

advanced.question: Section 1: Present Perfect. Focus on finished vs unfinished time, already, yet, just, for, since.
I ________ my homework already.
She ________ in this company for five years.

We ________ already eaten lunch.
He ________ been working here since January.
I ________ just finished my homework.

She ________ been to the new exhibition.
They ________ never visited that country before.
Section 2: Passive Voice. Choose the correct passive form.
The report ________ by the manager yesterday.
All the letters ________ before the meeting.
The homework ________ by the students last night.
The new bridge ________ in 2020.

The instructions ________ clearly explained.
The project ________ last year.
All the files ________ before the manager arrives.
The museum ________ every day.
The test ________ by the teacher before students enter.
The reports ________ submitted on time.
The package ________ yesterday.
The seminar ________ in the main hall.
Section 3: Conditionals Type 1 and Type 2. Choose the correct conditional form.
If it ________ tomorrow, we will stay home.
If I ________ enough money, I will buy a new laptop.
If she ________ now, she will catch the train.
If they ________ harder, they will pass the test.
If you ________ early, you will see him.
If I ________ more time, I would travel the world.
If she ________ taller, she would play basketball.
If we ________ rich, we would buy a big house.
If he ________ faster, he would have caught the bus.
If I ________ you, I would apologize.
If it ________ today, I will bring an umbrella.
If they ________ now, they will be late.
If I ________ taller, I could reach the shelf.
If she ________ harder, she could improve her grades.
If we ________ more careful, we wouldn't have mistakes.
If he ________ more polite, he would have more friends.
If they ________ earlier, they wouldn't miss the train.
If I ________ the answer, I would tell you.
If she ________ the instructions carefully, she wouldn't make mistakes.
If we ________ more organized, the project would be easier.
Section 4: Relative Pronouns. Choose who, which, where, whose, or that.
The man ________ helped me is a doctor.
The book ________ I borrowed is very interesting.
The house ________ we visited was huge.
The teacher ________ teaches us English is kind.
The city ________ I was born is very small.
I know a man ________ can speak five languages.
Technology ________ improves our lives is important.
The company ________ employs me is very large.
Education is something ________ everyone should have.
Work is a place ________ people spend many hours.
The student ________ won the scholarship is my friend.
The movie ________ we watched was amazing.
The teacher ________ helped me yesterday is very kind.
I visited the town ________ my grandmother lives.
She likes books ________ explain abstract ideas clearly.
This is the man ________ car was stolen last week.
The teacher ________ class I attended was strict.
I know the person ________ can solve this problem.
The school ________ I studied in was huge.
This is the building ________ was destroyed in the fire.
Section 5: Vocabulary. Work, Education, Technology, and Abstract Ideas.
She received a ________ for her excellent performance.
This software will ________ your computer speed.
The new device will ________ communication easier.
Critical thinking is a skill ________ all students should develop.
The manager gave a ________ speech about productivity.
Artificial intelligence is a branch of ________.
Work experience helps develop practical ________.
Education opens doors to new ________.
The meeting was postponed due to ________ circumstances.
She solved the problem using a very ________ idea.
He is responsible for managing ________.
Learning new technology can ________ your career.
The teacher explained an ________ concept clearly.
Students need to develop problem-solving ________.
She works in the field of ________ innovation.
He gave a very ________ presentation about science.
The company uses software to ________ efficiency.
She showed great ________ in solving the problem.
Technology ________ our lives in many ways.
Education is the key to unlocking ________.
